rulings.Case Title: CARISSA COPELAND VS. MOGOS BERHE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Post-Judgment Request For Production Of Documents To Judgment Debtor Desoto Cab Co. Dba Flywheel Taxi; Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $11,587.50
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery / Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, Mar-17-2026. LINE 1. 2-PLAINTIFF CARISSA COPELAND's Motion To Compel Responses To Post-Judgment Request For Production Of Documents To Judgment Debtor Desoto Cab Co. Dba Flywheel Taxi; Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $11,587.50. Hearing required. Copeland must bring to the hearing a list stating succinctly and without argument all issues that she believes need to be resolved on this motion. DeSoto must bring to the hearing all documents requested by the document requests that it has "withheld pursuant to proper objections" as stated on the seventh page of its opposition memorandum. Other than DeSoto's withholding of documents per its claimed proper objections, which the court is unable to decide without knowing what the withheld documents are, and the parties' requests for sanctions, the court is not able to determine what issues need to be resolved on this motion. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HEK)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Depositions Of Sharon Patrick, Rn, And Of Yuan-Da Fan, Md; Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery / Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, Mar-17-2026. LINE 15. 5-PLAINTIFFS JANE DOE, and JOHN DOES'S Motion To Compel The Depositions Of Sharon Patrick, Rn, And Of Yuan-Da Fan, Md; Request For Sanctions. Plaintiffs Jane and John Doe's motion to compel depositions of Sharon Patrick and Yuan-Da Fan is granted in part. Plaintiffs may take a deposition of Dr. Fan and Ms. Patrick of no more than three hours, excluding breaks and attorney colloquy, of each deponent that must occur on a mutually convenient date no later than March 26, 2026. Plaintiffs have shown good cause for these depositions and the Sutter defendants' arguments opposing the depositions lack merit. Due to the imminence of the trial and plaintiffs' delay in noticing the depositions, the depositions will be limited to three hours each. Because the Sutter defendants did not act without substantial justification, plaintiffs' request for sanctions is denied. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery / Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, Mar-17-2026. LINE 15. 3-PLAINTIFF JANE DOE'S NMotion To Compel Defendant Sutter Bay Hospitals Further Responses To Plaintiff Jane Does Request For Production Of Documents, Set One; Request For Sanctions. Plaintiff Jane Doe's motion to compel further responses from defendant Sutter Bay Hospitals to her set one requests 6, 10 and 13 for documents is denied in its entirety. Request 10 is denied as moot based on Sutter Bay's compliance with that request. Requests 6 and 13 are denied because Sutter Bay is not required to respond to those requests per Evidence Code 1157. rulings.Case Title: JANE DOE ET AL VS. SUTTER HEALTH, A CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: HEARING ON DEFENDANTS' APPLICATION SEEKING AN ORDER TO REOPEN DISCOVERY, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, AN ORDER SHORTENING TIME FOR A HEARING ON DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO REOPEN DISCOVERY
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery / Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, Mar-17-2026. LINE 15. 2-HEARING ON DEFENDANTS' EX PARTE APPLICATION SEEKING AN ORDER TO REOPEN DISCOVERY, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, AN ORDER SHORTENING TIME FOR A HEARING ON DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO REOPEN DISCOVERY. Defendants Sutter Health and Sutter Bay Hospitals' ex parte application to reopen discovery or, in the alternative, for an order shortening time for a hearing on a motion to reopen discovery is denied in its entirety. This case has been pending too long and the trial is too imminent to allow for a hearing on a motion to conduct an eleventh and a half hour independent medical examination of plaintiff Jane Doe which would be severely prejudicial to Jane Doe because she did not designate an expert to rebut the opinions resulting from that examination and may not even receive the results of the examination and the opinions therefrom until just before trial or possibly even after the start of trial. In short, defendants fail to adequately justify their delay in seeking an IME of Jane Doe and Jane Doe has provided an ample showing that she would be prejudiced by the allowing of an IME at this stage of the case. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(3012/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: PERRY ZHANG ET AL VS. SPROUTS FARMERS MARKET, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT On Plaintiffs' Complaint, Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declarations In Support Of Motion
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery / Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, Mar-17-2026. LINE 6. DEFENDANT SF MARKETS, LLC'S, DBA SPROUTS FARMERS MARKET (ERRONEOUSLY SUED HEREIN AS "SPROUTS FARMERS MERKET, INC."), MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT On Plaintiffs' Complaint, Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication. Defendant SF MARKETS, LLC dba SPROUTS FARMERS MARKET'S motion for summary judgment and alternative motion for summary adjudication as to all three causes of action alleged against it in the complaint filed by plaintiffs Perry Zhang and Yan Duan are denied in their entirety. Plaintiffs allege that Sprouts breached its duty of care because it failed to install vehicle barriers (bollards) in front of the entrance of its market. Sprouts contends that it owed no duty to do so and notes that the landlord (Byer) controlled the common area where the accident occurred. ***THE COURT'S COMPLETE TENTATIVE RULING HAS BEEN EMAILED TO THE PARTIES*** For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) rulings.Case Title: TRAVIS FIELDS VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO POLICE DEPARTMENT
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Adjudication And Declaration Of Travis Fields
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery / Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, Mar-17-2026. LINE 8. PLAINTIFF TRAVIS FIELDS' Motion For Summary Adjudication. Plaintiff Travis Fields' motion for summary adjudication is denied. There is a disputed material issue whether Mr. Fields had a sufficient property interest in the Audi to give him standing to contest the seizure and sale of the Audi which precludes entry of summary adjudication in Mr. Fields' favor. rulings.Case Title: ARTSIOM KHATKEVICH VS. HANNA KHATKEVICH
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery / Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, Mar-17-2026. LINE 11. DEFENDANT HANNA KHATKEVICH's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST Amended COMPLAINT. Defendant Hanna Khatkevich's anti-SLAPP motion to strike both claims alleged in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Artsiom Khatkevich is denied as to both claims. Artsiom's request for fees and costs is denied. Assuming without deciding that Hanna satisfied her burden of showing that Artsiom's two claims arise from activity protected by the anti-SLAPP statute, Artsiom has shown that neither of his claims are barred by the litigation privilege and both of the claims have minimal merit, thereby satisfying his burden of showing that his claims have the requisite probability of being proven and requiring that the motion must be denied. The claims are not barred by the litigation privilege because most or all of the allegations of wrongdoing by Hanna occurred outside of or in anticipation of litigation and either pre-dated Hanna's filing of an application for a domestic violence restraining order or post-dated the decision on her restraining order application. The declarations submitted by Artsiom show that he has admissible evidence showing that most or all of the conduct attributed to Hanna that is the basis for Artsiom's claims did in fact occur. Artsiom is not awarded fees or costs in opposing this motion because his opposition papers were not filed by an attorney and the motion was neither frivolous nor brought for an improper purpose. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HEK)
